entry.title: Re: AI/ML Functionality in SAP IBP Inventory Optimization
entry.content:
There are only ML Scenarios
Automated Outlier Correction:

  • Using algorithms like Isolation Forest (IForest) and DBSCAN, this functionality detects anomalies in sales history and adjusts forecasts dynamically, reducing manual data cleansing effort

Demand Sensing and Forecasting:

  • AI/ML algorithms like Gradient Boosting of Decision Trees (GBDT) and xGBoost are used to improve demand forecasting accuracy and reduce stockouts

entry.title: Issue in EA22 RTP analysis tab - Duplicate timeslice
entry.content:
Hello folks,

I can see duplicate timeslices (with same KWH value but different period) for On peak bucket while performing RTP analysis through EA22. What could be the reason for the same? Everything looks fine in billing document, customer didn't get overcharged. 

No duplicates are found for off peak bucket.

 

Any help would be greatly appreciated!!!

Utilities

entry.title: Re: Unable to close project / WBS
entry.content: Yes that is checked. The Final flag is set.

entry.title: Re: SHDB transaction is not checking S_BDC_MONI
entry.content: Use the authorization trace, do a recording and see which exact authorization objects are checked. Then compare the checks between the two systems to see what is different.

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Challenge - SAP Community Escape House - UI5
entry.content: Finally escaped UI5 room.

entry.title: Re: Does IAG support Integration with Blackline
entry.content:
We do not support the direct integration with backline.

We do have SCIM integration if backline is SCIM enabled. You may have to check with backline team.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CLOUD_IDENTITY_ACCESS_GOVERNANCE/e12d8683adfa4471ac4edd40809b9038/5552327c065a49dbbf1988e112f65633.html?version=CLOUDFOUNDRY

entry.title: Re: Extending Server-Side Caching not working
entry.content: Can you share more details about your implementation and how did you try to access your cache?

entry.title: Re: IBP - Last Value in Period when Mid-Period
entry.content:
Hi @willsuus ,

 

Perhaps you can try creating a third calculated key figure that:

- displays the LPA key figure if the month is in the past

- displays the LAST_VALUE key figure during the current month. Aggregation should be Sum.

Regards,

Andrei

https://api.sap.com/bointerface/I_PURCHASEORDERTP_2

 

Thanks

Vincent

entry.title: Unable to Access Sandbox Subscription APIs – Getting ECONNRESET Error
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently facing an issue while trying to access Sandbox Subscription APIs. It was working fine earlier, but recently, the API calls started failing with the error:

ECONNRESET

Here’s what I’ve tried so far:

Verified API key
Checked if the sandbox environment is active
Ensured correct API endpoint usage
Tried from multiple networks and devices
Despite these efforts, the issue persists. Has anyone else encountered this? Any insights or workarounds would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite: Storing SAP CPI Message Processing Logs in SAP HANA Cloud for Long Term Reten
entry.content:
Hi, 

How much did it cost you (HANA DB cost)?

It seems the biggest disadvantage of this solution is that it's too costly to stand up HANA DB and therefore does not provide good value for money against the purpose it solves. (logging for later analysis) 

There are better Azure / 3rd party solutions that are more cost effecient. 

Thanks 

Saurabh

Hi,

Please check the following community posting and hope it helps,

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/create-selection-variants-for-payment-medium-formats-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13388100

Kind Regards

Lucy

entry.title: Re: Bar chart in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi @LudoNoens 
Can you please provide me a solution for this.??

Thank You.

entry.title: Re: SAP B1: Can I add a column to Inventory Status (Avail...
entry.content:
Adding your own menu item is probably easier, or at least it will cause you less headaches. Also, it would allow you to simply show the result of the query that you already created.

Adding a field to an existing report, in any way, will make it slow, because what the GUI will do is something along these lines:

  1. Run the system query
  2. Draw the empty form that is going to show the result of the query, including the placeholder for your user defined graphic elements (the textbox or gridcolumn or whatever)
  3. Draw the grid with the result from the system query
  4. Then one row at a time:
  5. Run your query to get the extra data
  6. draw your user defined graphic element, with the result of step 5.

So any solution that runs the entire query in one go, will be considerably faster.

entry.title: Re: Mass changes to purchasing info record
entry.content:
Hello Raymond,

 

the error was like 

INSERT A MEDICATION IN THE COMPONENT MM-PUR-GF-MAS it showed as error .

entry.title: Re: Send reminder notification to manager after 48 hours...
entry.content:
Hi Sanjeet,

Thanks for the reply, could you help us if we have any custom solution to do so. Thanks

entry.title: Re: Enabling WEBGUI Firefighter in SAP GRC
entry.content:
Dear @sandyj,

Firefighter logon is a kind of framework for create a session with a Firefighter ID and then collect the standard log sources which was generated by the various application during the Session. What happens and what application do you reach during this session is not belong to the Firefighter topic. Firefighter ID in this sense is just a normal user whose session is initiated from the Firefighter Logon pad instead of typing a password or use an SSO, and the end of the session is made by using the logoff functionality. I'm not an expert of BW4WEB transaction hence I don't have any insight about how it works what UI technology it uses and how need it to be configured. I suggest to create a Customer Case in the respective component (BW4-*). 

Br, Mate

Dear requester,

I would highly recommend to register as we as SAP do  offer Webinars in Form customizations for our Product SAP Business ByDesign and also Cloud for customer.

To do so please open the below Link:

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/print-form-adaptation-with-adobe-livecycle-designer-for-sap-sales-and-service-cloud 

Felix was a form designer for may years and is happy to share also tips and tricks in form customizations.

Best regards

 

Didi

entry.title: Re: Data import of Local Currency only in the measure bas...
entry.content: it's depending on how you define your upload job. Simple way would be have to different upload job, one for actual(two measures) and one for plan( one measure). Or you can also create an empty column in upload file for plan data which can be mapped to GC.

entry.title: Re: Legacy data transfer and Make company code settings -...
entry.content:
Hi,

thank you NorbertB for a very good and clear reply.  

Our customer has now tested this and reports as follows.

To cumulative values they put the situation of 31.7 in ECC and to posted values (area32)1.8.2024-28.2.2025 book depreciation. 

The result is that S4 posts book depreciations for 01.08.2024-28.02.2025 (go-live in TEST 1.3.2025) to period 1 and in period 2 S4 is correcting the depreciation from previous period. 

Is this how you meant to use the dates and cumulative/posted values? 

Thanks for your comments again,

-ME-

entry.title: Re: No BAPI/FM to return complete list of Notifications?

entry.title: Re: crystal reports 2016: merge data across two (BTrieve)...
entry.content:
Dear@imgodot ,

Under Report options, enable the option 'Perform Grouping on Server'.

This should ignore the warning message in crystal report.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Update the dimension member in a transactional record...
entry.content:
Hi @saikhancs35 ,

using advanced formulas it could look like this.

Regards,

Thomas

PS: Cloud also be solve with a data action copy step and a delete statment in a following visual advanced formula step.

VARIABLEMEMBER #T1 OF [d/Version]

DATA([d/Version] = #T1, [d/DimA] = "MD01") = RESULTLOOKUP()
DELETE()
DATA() = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = #T1)

entry.title: Install SAP HANA Express Edition on openSUSE Leap as test or development environment
entry.content:
last updated: 2025-03-05

Introduction
I have been working on my pre-configured virtual machine since 2021, which has helped me gain a lot of useful knowledge. The pre-configured VM was a huge help for me, so I did not have a task to install SAP HANA Express Edition without the proper background. I was able to use the VM right away. My goal was to maintain the environment and constantly update it with newer versions.

Information
All necessary information belongs to SAP HANA Express Edition can be available in the next SAP Knowledge Base Article:

  • 3573213 - SAP HANA Express Edition - Central KBA

Dilemma
However, I ran into difficulties. The pre-installed operating system was SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 12 SP2, with the initial SAP HANA Express Edition 2.00.054. I was able to upgrade SAP HANA Express Edition a few times, but I also ran into a limitation.

The last time when I tried to update SAP HANA Express Edition 2.00.076.00 I received error message: 

14:49:09.873 - ERR : Linux version not supported: SLES 12.2
14:49:09.873 - ERR : Minimal SLES release required: SLES 12 SP3

I tried to walk around the possibilities of upgrading the OS, but it turned out that also not that easy. The operating system must be registered, and upgrading an old version of SLES can only be done offline (step by step). Further information (official site): Upgrading SUSE Linux Enterprise

Decision
I decided to achieve my goal, which is an environment completely my own, where I have no limits, difficulties, or constraints. If I want to maintain my environment for a long time, I need to be able to update the system at both the OS and SAP HANA database levels.

Solution
The SAP HANA Express Edition has a Binary Installer Method where the product can be installed manually, so this is different than the Virtual Machine Method. The installation can be done on an open source Linux operating system (openSUSE Leap).

Additionally
There is an official communication about SAP HANA Express Edition Virtual Machine Method only available until SAP HANA, Express Edition SPS 07.

Architecture
In my scenario I am using Windows 11 operating system on my laptop. 

Configuration

  • Memory: Laptop has 32 GB physical memory. Virtual Machine has 16 GB memory in general. During the install/upgrade of SAP HANA Express Edition it can be setup up to 24 GB memory (to be on safe side).
  • Disk: Virtual Machine has 150 GB disk.

Prerequisites

  • Hypervisor (VMware Workstation Player or Pro)
  • or any other preferred tool

Task
The task is to setup a complete environment from scratch.

1. The openSUSE Distributions - https://get.opensuse.org/ where you can download the operating system installer.

2. To install openSUSE Leap. I also used the following helps:
suse.com - openSUSE Leap: Step-by-Step Guide for installing it on your virtualized environment
youtube - How to Install OpenSUSE Leap 15.5 Server

3. SSH test (based on IP address), further configuration later (e.g. host file).

4. Maintain packages with Zypper package manager.
--Zypper commands--
sudo zypper refresh
sudo zypper update
sudo zypper search OpenJDK | grep Runtime
--These packages needs to be checked/installed--
sudo zypper install libgcc_s1 libstdc++6
--These packages needs to be checked/installed--
sudo zypper install insserv libatomic1 libnuma1 numactl
--Check versions of the packages--
rpm -q libgcc_s1 libstdc++6 libatomic1

5. Maintain proper hostname. This is important to setup fully qualified domain name, otherwise the SAP HANA Express Edition installer will be fail with the following error: Build process on webide fails: Error occured during communication with HTTP server. I used the following hostname: 'opensuse.example.com'.
--Check hostname--
hostname
--Setup /etc/hosts-
sudo nano /etc/hosts

--Setup /etc/hostname-
sudo nano /etc/hostname

6. Restart the Linux system.
--Command--
sudo reboot

Remark: previously I experienced issue with SAP HANA Express Edition upgrade and I needed to setup the available memory of the environment to 24 GB to run it smoothly. So I setup this number during the installation process.

7. To install SAP HANA Express edition. I also used the following helps:
youtube - How-To / Step-by-Step Install SAP HANA DB on openSUSE for FREE
I have created the following folder for the installer files.
--Enter to home directory--
cd /home/<username>
--Make directory Downloads folder /home/<username>/Downloads--
mkdir Downloads

I have download all files with SAP HANA, Express Edition Download Manager (downloaded version 2.00.076.00.20240701.1). This was the tool which I used previously to download the pre-configured VM image, but it can be used for downloading the binary installer also.

I have copied the files to the proper folder of the linux environment and extracted them.
--Extract files--
tar -xvzf hxe.tgz
tar -xvzf hxexsa.tgz
tar -xvzf sdi.tgz

--Install the SAP HANA Express Edition--
sudo ./setup_hxe.sh

I left all options at default during the installation process (didn't change anything).

8. Maintain proper hostname on Windows environment. The hosts file can be found in the folder C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc. This needs to be maintained.

Conclusion
It is possible to have a fully managed environment (including the operating system) that is maintainable, upgradeable and without restrictions. This setup can be used for long time not only a short period. The created environment is fully suitable for learning, testing and other (development) purposes. In nutshell:

  • The OS is a free distribution, so it can be installed, updated without restriction.
  • With the package manager the necessary packages can be downloaded, installed, updated.
  • The OS fully supports the SAP HANA Express Edition product.

Question
Q&A link for SAP HANA, express edition: https://answers.sap.com/tags/73555000100800000651

Release Information

Release DateDescription
2025.03.05First/initial Release of the SAP Blog Post documentation (Product Information).

entry.title: Client Copy in SAP ECC vs. SAP S/4HANA: Key Differences
entry.content:
Client Copy is a fundamental process in SAP that allows administrators to replicate client-specific data within the system. While the concept remains similar in both SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA, there are significant differences in execution, available tools, and underlying database technologies.

1. Understanding Client Copy in SAP ECC

SAP ECC (ERP Central Component) uses traditional database structures and methodologies for client copy. The process involves copying all or specific parts of client-dependent data within the same system or across different systems. This copy procedure usually runs in the target client.

Types of Client Copy in ECC

  • Local Client Copy: Copies data within the same SAP system.

  • Remote Client Copy: Copies data from one SAP system to another.

  • Client Export/Import: Exports client data to a file and imports it into another system.

Tools/T-Codes Used in SAP ECC

  • Transaction SCCL (Local Client Copy)

  • Transaction SCC9 (Remote Client Copy)

  • Transaction SCC8/SCC7 (Client Export/Import)

  • Transaction SCC5 (Delete Client)
  • Transaction SCC3 (Client Logs)

Challenges in ECC Client Copy

  • Time-consuming due to table-based copy processes.

  • High database resource consumption is affecting system performance.

  • Lack of native HANA optimization, leading to slower data processing.

  • Larger data footprint due to traditional relational database structures.

2. Client Copy in SAP S/4HANA

SAP S/4HANA, built on the HANA database, brings performance improvements and simplified data structures, impacting how client copy operations work. The new client copy tool comes with a task list and a new log, an ALV-based table instead of log files, which allows the administrator to handle tasks efficiently and allows the administrator to perform from a third client, such as 000 instead of source/target clients.

We can copy a table or package from one client to another with SCCLN without a pre- or post-processing step.

Key Differences in S/4HANA

  • Optimized for HANA: uses in-memory processing, speeds up data copy operations, and SCCLN is almost 10x faster than SCCL.

  • Simplified Data Model: redundant and aggregated tables (such as indexes, totals, and history tables) are removed, reducing the volume of copied data.

  • Selective Data Transition: offers better filtering options for copying only necessary data.

  • Better Performance: Uses parallel processing and advanced algorithms to reduce runtime and resource consumption.

Tools Used in SAP S/4HANA

  • Transaction SCCLN (Local Client Copy, still applicable but optimized)

  • Transaction SCC9N (Remote Client Copy with better performance)

  • Client Export/Import (SCC8N/SCC7N): Improved with reduced data redundancy

  • Client Deletion (SCC5N): You must initiate the client deletion from a different client, not the one you want to delete, unlike the older SCC5. 
  • Client Logs (SCC3N): Detailed logs and optimizations for handling large datasets; better performance with the HANA database.

Advantages of Client Copy in S/4HANA

  • Faster execution times due to HANA’s in-memory processing.

  • Reduced database footprint with an optimized data model.

  • Improved flexibility with better data selection and replication options.

  • Lower system impact, making it feasible to perform copies without significant downtime.

3. Comparison Table: SAP ECC vs. SAP S/4HANA Client Copy

Feature SAP ECC SAP S/4HANA

DatabaseTraditional RDBMS (e.g., Oracle, MS SQL)SAP HANA (In-Memory)
PerformanceSlower, resource-intensiveFaster, optimized for HANA
Data Model ComplexityComplex, redundant tablesSimplified, redundant-free model
Execution TimeLonger processing timeFaster due to parallel processing using table splitting
Downtime ImpactHigher impactLower impact
Filtering OptionsLimitedAdvanced selection options
T-Codes Used

SCCL, SCC9, SCC8, SCC7,

SCC3,SCC5

SCCLN, SCC9N, SCC8N, SCC7N,

SCC3N, SCC5N

4. Conclusion

Client Copy remains a critical operation in both SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA, but with significant improvements in the latter. SAP S/4HANA’s in-memory architecture, simplified data model, and enhanced processing capabilities make client copy operations more efficient and less disruptive. Organizations planning to migrate to S/4HANA can benefit from these optimizations for faster and smoother system administration tasks.
Please do let me know your feedback on this. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: In GB, there is no validation for item category when...
entry.content: ideally you can check if feature is enabled with customer support through a ticket, otherwise you can give me the realm details and i can take a look as one-off

entry.title: 回复： End-to-End Visual Inspection Process in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content: very Nice!

entry.title: Re: Compare two clients (SAP environments) in SAP Signavio
entry.content:
Hello Jessica,

I understand the following situation : 

There are 2 separate SAP ERP Systems that both belong to the same company. You have usage rights for SIGNAVIO Process Insights.

In this case, you can connect both ERP systems to Process Insights, yet the data will be shown separately. There is a toggle switch on the lower left hand side of the UI where you can choose the SID of the system. Therefore, you can only look at one system at a time.

This changes when you are using Plug and Gain in combination with SIGNAVIO Process Intelligence. As Plug and Gain leverages the data from Process Insights, we can combine the data of multiple Process Instance Instances into one Eventlog. As a result, you will see data from both ERP systems. Hope this answer helps,

Andreas

entry.title: Re: OCC extension and cache manager
entry.content:
Hello,

I check that comment, but it's a general reference about how to add caching in extension. So this is not something that will work with your Controllers in OCC extension, as I can see so far.

Currently didn't know alternatives to suggested solution mentioned as accepted, see https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-q-a/occ-extension-and-cache-manager/qaq-p/12629078/comment-id/448778#M448778

From time when initially post this question use it at different project.

Best Regards,

Vitalii

entry.title: Value Driver Tree - Year on Year Node
entry.content:
Hello all, 

"I am encountering an issue while calculating Gross Sales for unbooked data in 2020 and 2021. The values do not appear when I try to increase or decrease the percentage of %Gross Sales for these years by a specific amount. This is because the percentage of Gross Sales is dependent on Gross Sales (as a child element) and also acts as a driver. In the updated version, the option to select the Year-on-Year node is no longer available, and the simulation feature is also missing. Could you please suggest a solution to retrieve these values under these constraints?

"Below is the screenshot showing that there are no values generated for 2020 and 2021."

this is how it should work

entry.title: Re: Table Input Format for Anomaly Detection in HANA_ML
entry.content:
Hi @mark_foerster The error "Input table 0: column 0 has invalid SQL type" typically means that one of of the columns has a type that is not supported by the algorithm. Here in the first table the first column.

The supported column types of the PAL algorithms are documented here https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud-database/sap-hana-cloud-sap-hana-database-predictive-analysis-library-pal/isolation-forest-isolation-forest-11345d9

Please check that the ID column is the first column and that the column type is supported. In case the column type is different a cast should help https://help.sap.com/doc/cd94b08fe2e041c2ba778374572ddba9/2024_4_QRC/en-US/hana_ml.dataframe.html#hana_ml.dataframe.DataFrame.cast

entry.title: Re: Assignment of incoterm locations in SAP TM
entry.content:
hi gojjir

Pl. check if the second stage has planning block. If yes, then it will not be planned. This can be controlled by settings in Logistics Integration Profile

Regards

Sandeep Patil

entry.title: Re: Two documents are saved instead of one
entry.content: Sorry for the late response, I missed your comment. In the link you send it written that ' Therefore any error message raised in the BTE has to be handled as A-message.' The problem with A-message is that it causes the screen to exit and the user must insert all the data again. Is there another solution? It is written that " There must be no error or warning messages issued when the BTE is called between the assignment of document numbers and the COMMIT WORK." Is it possible to interfere with the standard code before the assignment of document numbers?

entry.title: Job Family/unit details in PRD reports
entry.content:
Hello,

Created a custom registration report in PRD and there is a requirement to add Job Family details or Job unit details in the same report. Was using the table PA_JOB_FAMILY to fetch the required data but it results in empty table. When ask the same thing to SF agent they informed me that this table does not have any value as of now and I have raise an enhancement ticket for the same.

Doesn't it sounds weird. So, someone can please let me know if you also fetch job family details via some other table for your customer and if you can also help me with the same.

It would be very helpful. Many thanks in advance!
Jyoti 🙂

entry.title: Re: Automatic Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document
entry.content:
Hi there, You can leverage RPA or it is called BPA (business process automation) now to achieve the automation. Basically it is calling API (change inbound delivery) to fill the quantity in the PO. If you can develop your own program, it can also be achieved. 

Overview | Inbound Delivery (A2X) | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

entry.title: S/4 HANA public cloud be one of the Multi-ERP (FPC) architecture with Ariba instance
entry.content: Can Ariba integration for multi-ERP (Federated Process Control) architecture support as one of the backends  for S/4 Hana public cloud instance with other SAP back-ends technically feasible in Parent - Child Ariba realm setup?

workflow does not have approvers. It only has CC users and Employee & Manager are set up to receive a custom email. However emails are not getting trigger

 

Any help - thank you!

entry.title: Looking for New Version courses
entry.content:
Hello All, 

I noticed that several SAP courses are being retired in March, but I'm unable to find a consolidated list of these courses along with their new versions or alternatives.

Could someone kindly guide me if there is a CSV file or any reference guide available for this?

Thank you in advance. 

Regards

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting - New Balance Validation Process
entry.content:
Hi Srinivas.S,

Thanks.

Regarding "1", "2" and "3", you need to check this at your end for the roles. For. eg. the link that you have provided in "3" already have a pre-requisite.

"4" is regarding Line Validation which is out of scope of this blog.

For "5" the old apps are deprecated for GR for the current cloud release and will be removed in the upcoming release.

Regarding "6", you can refer to the snote mentioned in the blog regarding migration. The note has an attachment explaining it with the screenshot.

Regards,

Hari

entry.title: Monitoring End to End Master Data Replication using SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
In SAP, master data replication is crucial because it ensures consistent and accurate data across multiple systems within an organization, allowing for streamlined business processes by providing a single source of truth for critical information like customer details, product data, and vendor information, which is essential for accurate reporting, decision-making, and efficient operations across different departments.

The introduction of the new "Business Objects" page in SAP Cloud ALM's Integration & Exception Monitoring app provides customers transparency in the replication process of Master Data.

Customer Scenario

In a sample customer scenario, the customer creates a worker in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

 

According to the configuration, this worker needs to be replicated to the SAP Project and Resource Management, resource management capability service.

Replication configuration

 

Refer https://help.sap.com/docs/master-data-integration/sap-business-data-orchestration-8ce78b673ef04cc1bcfeb01c93ef7885/general-setup-of-sap-master-data-orchestration on how to configure the Business data orchestration.

Prerequisites

  • Ensure that you have the Integration Owner Sensitive role.
  • Ensure that all the involved managed components in the integration are configured, and data collection is active for all categories.
  • Ensure the SAP Master Data Integration service that functions as the MDI hub is configured. 
  • Ensure the master data replication process in the landscape you want to monitor is orchestrated using the SAP Master Data Integration service.

How to use Business Objects Page

  • Login to SAP Cloud ALM and launch the Integration and Exception Monitoring application.
  • Select the SAP Master Data Integration service in the scope selector.

 

  • Select the Business Objects Page.

 

  • You would get an overview of replications for different object types.

 

  • As we are interested in the object of type “Workforce Person”, refine the results by choosing the preferred filters for Business Object Type and choose Go.
  • The screen refreshes with the business objects based on the defined filters.

 

  • Choose the card name to view all the replication instances of the master data object.

 

  • You can view information that the Workforce person “ZADRAN” replication has failed.
  • Choose the object for more information. The details of the business object instance are displayed on the right pane.

 

  • You can view the information that the object replication is successful in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, which is the provider of the object.
  • The object replication is also successfully replicated in SAP Master Data Integration, which serves as the hub in the replication.
  • The object replication failed in the SAP Project and Resource Management, resource management capability, which is the consumer.
  • For additional information, choose “View additional information”

 

  • You can view that the replication failed since the Email ID was not maintained. To resolve the issue, you can now maintain the Email ID for the worker in SAP S/4HANA cloud to retrigger the replication process.

 

 

For more information about the monitoring master data replication, refer to:  https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/monitoring-master-data-replication

entry.title: How to resolve the "Save Failed" error in SAP Web Companion
entry.content:
I attempted to save a content in SAP Web Companion under Fieldglass, but I encountered a "Save Failed" error. Upon inspecting the issue, the preview tab displayed the message: "UE_CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION."

Does anyone know the possible reasons for this error or have experience resolving it?

SAP Enable Now

what is the approach are you refering to? Which will allow us to connect to the Azure Event Grid and push/get events between Event Mesh and Event Grid?

I got through entitlements and I can see event-hub (but it seems to be only SAP to SAP). Can you please elaborate more on how to connect with azure event grid?

Thanks

Vlado

entry.title: Re: How to handle errors for semantic key with number ran...
entry.content: Your scenario is best handled by deferring the number range assignment until after validations have passed. Instead of using a determination on save, use a late determination after successful validation to generate the semantic key. This ensures that numbers are only assigned when the transaction is committed, preventing gaps. Additionally, store errors in a buffer during validation and block the save if needed. This approach avoids losing number range values due to failed transactions.

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA public cloud be one of the Multi-ERP (FPC) a...
entry.content: Yes, Ariba integration for a Multi-ERP (Federated Process Control - FPC) architecture can support S/4HANA Public Cloud as one of the backends alongside other SAP backends in a Parent-Child Ariba Realm setup. However, technical feasibility depends on Ariba Cloud Integration Gateway (CIG) configurations, master data harmonization, and transaction orchestration across multiple ERPs. Ensure that Ariba Network and Procurement Operations can route transactions appropriately based on ERP assignment. Consulting SAP Ariba documentation and SAP support is recommended for detailed setup guidelines.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Planning Copy from Upload Version to Public Version: Overwrite issue
entry.content:
Hi , 

Thanks for your reply, I forgot to mention in advanced formula I had this configuration "CONFIG.GENERATE_UNBOOKED_DATA = OFF".

entry.title: Re: Table Input Format for Anomaly Detection in HANA_ML
entry.content:
@mark_foerster, your point is to what I understand, you trained with a table without ID column, you predict with a table without ID column?

If so

  • easiest would be to add_id to the fit dataframe
  • or drop the ID column from the predict dataframe, the latter I assume is what you would like to avoid.

Best regards

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting Evolution : Upcoming New Major Updates in 1H 2025 Releases
entry.content:
The Evolution of Recruitment: Why It Matters

Before diving into the upcoming features, it’s important to understand the broader trend in recruitment. As talent acquisition becomes more data-driven and candidate-centric, companies are looking for ways to simplify workflows while simultaneously enhancing the hiring experience. SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting has been continuously innovating to meet these needs, and 2025 will be no different.

The new features coming in  1H 2025 are designed to address both current industry demands and the future of recruiting — where Artificial Intelligence (AI), automation, data analytics, and mobile-first tools are key. With these updates, SAP SuccessFactors aims to help businesses improve their recruitment strategies and stay ahead in an increasingly competitive talent market.

1H 2025 Release: Innovations to Improve Recruitment Efficiency
The first half of 2025 will bring several key features focused on automation, candidate experience, and integrating AI to streamline recruitment. These innovations are designed to help recruitment teams reduce manual tasks, speed up hiring processes, and ultimately make better hiring decisions.

1. Career site Similar Jobs Component
As part of 1H 2025 release it will provide a specific component to add in career site builder which will help candidates to see similar jobs that may also be a good fit.
It will Identify similar jobs based on an automated matching algorithm and show to candidates

2. Resume upload Hero Component
One of the most exciting features in the 1H 2025 release to provide a customizable Hero Component for the AI skills resume upload match, in which IT will leverage AI and machine learning to better match the posted  jobs based on their skills, experience etc. mentioned in the resume and defined in Job Description of the posted Jobs.

3. New Reimagined Interface for Candidate Profile
In the 1H 2025 release, SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting will introduce new refreshed personalized candidate page. Which will help candidate to access their information in a neatly organized view, easy and intuitive to navigate relevant information and it will also ensure brand consistency with improved styling and theming.

4. New Candidate Job Apply Experience
As part of 1H 2025 release to provide, it will improve the the experiences Job Apply process to
better streamline the collection of applicant data, Here is some key benefits of this new apply process

  • Streamline the application process with a guided wizard tailored to meet your unique hiring needs
  • Dynamically capture applicant responses using branching questions
  • Ensure brand consistency with improved styling and theming

5. New Improvement in New Application Workbench
As part of 1H 2025 release, we will get a collection minor improvements to the new applicant workbench like 

  • Recruiting Team will able to contact agency instead of the applicant
  • Drag & Drop applicants into different statuses like old application dashboard
  • Print or save applications 
  • Access referral and agency information on the application detail page

6. AI - Assisted Interviews and Interview Feedback Summarization 
As part of 1H 2025 release is the enhancement of AI-powered, Generative AI Summaries of interviews and interview, here is below benefits of this feature

  • Review interviewer feedback with an AI consolidated summary
  • Gain additional perspectives on completed interviews with AI insights derived from the transcripts.
  • Manually adjust summaries as needed to ensure ethical evaluation of all interviewees

 

Preparing for the 1H 2025 SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting Updates

As we approach to 1H 2025, consultants and businesses should begin preparing for these exciting new features. Here are a few key recommendations:

  • Train your recruitment team: Ensure your recruitment team is up-to-date with the new features and understands how to make the most of AI-powered tools, mobile capabilities, and predictive analytics.
  • Review and refine your recruitment strategy: Align your recruitment strategy with the new features to enhance your workflows, streamline communication, and improve candidate experience.
  • Leverage SAP SuccessFactors’ robust reporting: Use the advanced analytics capabilities to continuously assess and optimize your recruitment processes for better outcomes.

 

Note: The above blog is based on latest announcements & upcoming features. For the latest updates, please refer to SAP’s official communications.

Looking forward to catching up with you soon with a new blog!

entry.title: Re: How to represent manual email one swimlane to another...
entry.content: As far as I know, message symbols do not have to be modelled within a pool, as the process flow already describes an information interaction. We use it for external interaction. Additionally, I accept the warning as it is only a warning. (You can also switch off the warning message in the modeling conventions.)

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs
entry.content:
Hello @jeetendrakapase 

wow, you really put a lot of effort into these Q&A! Very helpful. 😊

One question regarding No. 12, embedded SAC: I'm not sure where I read or heard it, but I think I understood that the embedded SAC in S/4 HANA Public Cloud would disappear sooner or later?

And my most anticipated question about the Foundation services, is unfortunately still not answered satisfactory 🤔 : Wow exactly will SAP handle the Data Delta Extraction technically, from S4 CDS Views into the Data Lake Files?

Will there be lots of Notes or a huge TCI be required to be implemented in S4 or will it be the same technology like today when e.g. using Replication Flows into SAP Datasphere? It would be really helpful if we could see a picture/slide about those technical details soon 🤗

Thank you very much.

Martin

entry.title: Re: How to run an alvGrid in mb51 using BDC?
entry.content:
Try to set opt-nobinpt to abap_true, you should also

  • Go to SPRO-Materials Management-Inventory and Physical Inventory Inventory-Reporting - Set up print functions for reporting and then read IM activity documentation (especially the GRID/LIST checkbox).
  • But as @Sandra_Rossi wrote don't expect to control the ALV Grid execution.

Note: The field is not a custom field but the standard 'Visit Type' field.

 

 

 

Saygılarımla,

Kadir

entry.title: Removing "View Checklist Result" from Learning History in SAP SF LMS
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I’m looking for guidance on a UI customization within the SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System. Specifically, is there any way to remove or hide the “View Checklist Result” from the learning history displayed in the user interface?

Background:

Scenario: Our end users are seeing the “View Checklist Result” in their learning history, and we’d prefer to streamline the interface by removing this element.

Attempts: I’ve reviewed the configuration settings and UI options available in the LMS administration, but haven’t found a direct way to disable or remove this view.

entry.title: Re: Table Input Format for Anomaly Detection in HANA_ML
entry.content:
Hello Andreas,

thanks a lot for your reply. I had already asked Claude, and got some very similar information. This is my base table for input:

This is the offending code:

j

 

I am really at a loss here. Just 4 data columns, all are of type DOUBLE. And the table starts with an integer column ID. And still HANA_ML complains about column 0 having the wrong SQL type. I am at a loss here what is causing this:

So I read the documentation and still it won't work. Is there a way to get more detailed infos from HANA_ML, like what is the unsupported SQL type it found?

Regards,

Mark

entry.title: Issue with BAPI_RESERVATION_CREATE1 – Reservation Not Visible in MB23
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have created a reservation for a material using BAPI_RESERVATION_CREATE1, and I received a success message. However, when I check in MB23, the reservation is not appearing.

Has anyone faced this issue before? Do I need to commit the transaction explicitly or check for any missing parameters?

Any guidance would be appreciated!

Thanks in advance

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud | Cost Estimation by Batch
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

My client (semiconductor manufacturing) is planning to use SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. There is a requirement where they wants to analyse costs and manage price according to batches.

This case applies to Make to Stock (MTS) business process.

For Example if I have 1 Semi-Finished Goods (SFG) A with two batches A.1, A.2. Each batch is manufactured with a different cost, due to batch A.2 consumed more raw materials/overhead costs than planned. 

Next these two batches A.1, A.2 are consumed in Production Orders to produce Finished-Goods (FG). Because these 2 SFG batches have different costs, the FG manufactured by these 2 batches have different costs as well.

And they want these costs to be reflected when they choose the FG to sell to customers - that is - when they choose the FG to sell, they can choose batches in this FG, with price of each batch referenced correctly from costs collected to manufacture them.

Currently I know if we use standard price for FG, the cost for all Production Orders will be collected in Pricediff, then distributed all to inventory/COGS based on quantity. All batches of 1 FG will have same price.

Is there a way to do this in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public? I tried to use this case Solved: Batch wise costing SAP public cloud - SAP Community but the links for costing don't work, and I know SAP has removed standard cost estimation by split valuation. Mixed costing does not work as well, since all batches they use are manufacturing batches.

Kindly enlighten me on this issue.

entry.title: Approval Process in Add to Learning History
entry.content:
Hi,

We would like to set up approval process when Users use the Add to Learning History and mark course completion. The approval is to be triggered to 2 different set of Approver group.

Example: There are Users of Location 'A' and Location 'B' with Approver 'A' and Approver 'B'

If Users of Location 'A' add the course to learning history, it should trigger approval to Approver 'A'. The Approver 'B' should not see the Users' 'A' approval requests. Likewise, if Users of Location 'B' add the course to learning history, it should trigger approval to Approver 'B'. The Approver 'A' should not see the Users' 'B' approval requests.

Any solution is appreciated. SAP SuccessFactors Learning 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Tenant migration made possible
entry.content:
Hi @Sycntegral ,

could you please clarify what you mean by:

  1. interdependencies between tables and APIs
  2. "how will this work?" If you have any specific question that is not covered in the blog, please specifiy it.
  3. The backup is the old tenant itself, as it holds all data needed. During the migration, we do not need a backup.

Thank you and BR,

Thore

entry.title: Prefilled Forms but different Matching ID
entry.content:
Hello,
 
We are acting in the B2B segment when it comes to sales and marketing processes. Videos/articles and white papers are made available via forms on our homepage.
 
To promote this gated content, we send emails to targeted contacts. The forms are pre-filled for more convenience and to provide a better customer journey. For this we use the “Outbound ID” function which is enabled on the url itself. 
 
Our current problem is that some customers/contacts forward the URL or email and other people (with other personal details) fill out the form. Due to the outbound ID, these two contacts will now be merged. Usually the orignal contacts gets a new name and email for their best record. 
 
I would now like to know how and whether there are other solutions, for example that the SMC on forms can be checked with the email, regarding match and merge logic. 
 
Does anyone here have ideas or feedback? How have you solved this problem? 
 
BR 
Sascha

entry.title: 在进行应收汇票的批量认领导入时，提示抵销科目的问题，提示未在公司代码中定义，请指教下，如何处理的

entry.content: thank you

entry.title: Re: Log for Financial Plan Data upload
entry.content:
Dear @Annika98, 

please note that the Import Financial Plan Data app serves only as an interface for uploading data into the system. It is designed specifically for administrators, and all data must be manually reviewed and maintained before uploading. While the tool includes technical checks, such as verifying the existence of master data in the target system, it does not incorporate business logic or audit functionalities.

If you require data audits, we recommend using the planning functionality available in SAP Analytics Cloud.
For details, see Auditing Activity.

Best regards
Susanne
